definition: Term premium is structurally rebuilding through additive supply-side (fiscal issuance, dealer SLR constraints) and demand-side (reserve de-accumulation, petrodollar recycling decline, Chinese holdings reduction) channels, with adjusted levels likely at 80-130bp.
evidence: Supply: $2T+/year net issuance, quarterly refunding moving 10Y 10-20bp
Demand: foreign official holdings declined from ~33% to ~23% of outstanding
Basis trade compresses measured term premium 15-25bp below fair value
open_questions: ACM-Kim-Wright model gap of 30-50bp makes true term premium unobservable
rates_strategist_01 self-rates measurement confidence at only 6/10
How much of 5Y5Y elevation is embedded term premium vs. genuine r-star repricing?
